name: mongo
explanation: MongoDB's default port (27017) is universal and the wire protocol is detectable. Strictly per spec, only `hosts` is required, so an unauthenticated localhost dev instance could be discovered generically. In practice, every production MongoDB enables authentication and most also require a dedicated `datadog` user with `clusterMonitor` role, plus a `connection_scheme` decision (`mongodb` vs. `mongodb+srv`) for replica sets. Classification is custom because of the auth-mode branching, but in real deployments it falls through to credentials-required.
